expressions.english: long hair
expressions.notes:
긴다 = to be long
긴 is the adjective form of 길다.

expressions.english: short hair
expressions.notes:
짧다 = to be short
짧은 is the adjective form of 짧다.

expressions.english: to wash one's hair
to be smart
to comb one's hair
to have a headache
to get a haircut
to clear one's head, rest one's mind
Watch your head.
I want to grow my hair long.
I want to dye my hair.
I wash my hair in the morning.
I have a headache, so I can't sleep.
expressions.notes:
-이/어/여서 = because, since
잠 = sleep
오다 = to come
잠이 오다 = to be sleepy; to feel drowsy
잠이 안 오다 = to be unable to sleep

expressions.english: I had a headache, so I took some pills.
expressions.notes:
약 = pill, medicine
약을 먹다 = to take pills/medicine

expressions.english: My brain is slow today.
expressions.notes:
오늘 = today
잘 = well; carefully
머리가 안 돌아기다 = one's brain is slow, cannot think very quickly

expressions.english: I'm thinking of getting a haircut.
expressions.notes: -(으)ㄴ 생각 중이다 = to be thinking of + V-ing

expressions.english: I like girls with long hair.
If you are not smart, your body will pay for it.
expressions.notes:
나쁘다 = to be bad
머리가 나쁘다 = to be not smart; to be slow in understanding
손 = hand
발 = foot
고생 = hardship, trouble

expressions.korean: 그 사람은 정말 머리가 좋은 것 같아요.
expressions.english: I think he's really smart.

expressions.korean: 잠깐 밖에 나가서 머리 좀 식히고 올게요.
expressions.english: I will go out for a bit and get some fresh air.
expressions.notes:
잠깐 = short time; for a moment
밖 = outside
나가다 = to go out
식히다 = to cool off
